items_en: 1. Welcomes the establishment at United Nations Headquarters of the Arche of Return, a permanent memorial to honour the victims of slavery and the transatlantic slave trade, in a prominent location to which delegations, United Nations staff and visitors can easily access, and which is built to recall this tragedy and to highlight the legacy of slavery and the transatlantic slave trade;

2. Expresses its appreciation to the Committee of Interested States representing all regions of the world, which includes Brazil, Ghana, Jamaica, Kenya, the Netherlands, Portugal, Qatar, Senegal, Suriname and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, in which the Member States of the Caribbean Community and the African Union play a leading role, in collaboration with the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization and representatives of the Secretariat and civil society, and which led the permanent memorial project;

3. Recalls the establishment of a trust fund for the permanent memorial known as the United Nations Trust Fund for Partnerships - Permanent Memorial, administered by the United Nations Office for Partnerships, and takes note of the current level of contributions to the Trust Fund;
4. Expresses its sincere appreciation to those Member States, organizations and individuals that have made contributions to the Fund;

5. Takes note of the report of the Secretary-General on the educational programme of action on the transatlantic slave trade and slavery 3 , in which the Secretary-General reviews the various aspects of the strategy to inform future generations about the causes, consequences, lessons learned and sequelae of the slave trade, to educate them and to make them aware of the dangers of racism and prejudice, and encourages further action;

6. Requests the Secretary-General to continue to organize annually a series of activities to commemorate the International Day of Remembrance of the Victims of Slavery and the Transatlantic Slave Trade, including a commemorative meeting of the General Assembly at United Nations Headquarters and, as appropriate, activities through the network of United Nations information centres;

7. Requests the Department of Public Information of the Secretariat, in cooperation with interested countries and relevant United Nations bodies and bodies, to continue to take appropriate measures to raise awareness among the public around the world of commemorative activities and the permanent memorial erected at United Nations Headquarters;

items_en: 8. Reiterates its request to Member States, in accordance with General Assembly resolution 64/15 of 16 November 2009, to develop, in accordance with their national legislation, educational programmes aimed at raising awareness and understanding among future generations, including in school curricula, of the teachings, history and consequences of slavery and the slave trade, and to inform the Secretary-General accordingly for the purpose of reporting thereon in his report;

9. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly at its seventy-third session on the measures taken to continue the implementation of the programme of educational action, including measures adopted by Member States to implement the present resolution, as well as on efforts to raise awareness of commemorative activities and the permanent memorial among the public throughout the world;

10. Requests the United Nations Office for Partnerships to submit to the General Assembly at its seventy-first session, through the Secretary-General, a comprehensive report on the status of the Fund, indicating in particular the contributions received and the use made of the Fund;
11. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its seventy-first session an item entitled " commemoration of the abolition of slavery and the transatlantic slave trade".
